title: Uniform Circular Motion
content:
Uniform Circular Motion

Uniform circular motion occurs when an object moves in a circle at constant speed. Even though the speed is constant, the direction of velocity changes, so the object is accelerating.

Centripetal Acceleration

The acceleration that keeps an object moving in a circle is called centripetal acceleration, directed toward the center of the circle:

$$a_c = rac{v^2}{r}$$

Where $v$ is the speed and $r$ is the radius.

Centripetal Force

The net force causing centripetal acceleration is called centripetal force:

$$F_c = m a_c = m rac{v^2}{r}$$

Period and Frequency

The period $T$ is the time for one revolution. The frequency $f$ is the number of revolutions per second:

  • $T = rac{2\pi r}{v}$
  • $f = rac{1}{T}$

Example Problem

A 1 kg mass moves in a circle of radius 2 m at 4 m/s. Find the centripetal acceleration and force.

  • $a_c = rac{4^2}{2} = 8$ m/s²
  • $F_c = 1 imes 8 = 8$ N
